title: W0 Classification artifact
description: The single artifact written by the W0 Classify node (spec/classification.json). Routes the whole game-omni pipeline: physics-first archetype, one-line core loop, and explicit scope-cut. Authored by packages/skills/classify-game/SKILL.md.

prompt.description: The raw args.prompt, copied verbatim. Provenance for every downstream node.

archetype.description: Physics-first routing key. W2 scaffolds templates/modules/<archetype>. Closed set — never invented.

coreLoop.description: One self-enclosed sentence: player verb + goal/win + obstacle + fail/reset. The spine W1 elaborates and the seed for W1 win/lose.

coreVerb.description: The single central player verb (e.g. jump, push, place tower).

physicsProfile.description: The structured physics that justifies the archetype (auditable, drives W2 engine params).

reasoning.description: 1-2 sentences: which physics KEY QUESTION decided the archetype. On low confidence, names the ambiguity / runner-up.

confidence.description: high = unambiguous; medium = resolved via tie-break; low = barely fit / defaulted (see SKILL §5).

scoringModel.description: The scoring signal derived from the prompt's GOAL-TYPE (SKILL §2.5), passed to W1 so it scores only when the type warrants it (never a reflexive open counter). none = pure completion, no countable rewards (win is the readout); bounded-collectible = completion via a finite reward set (maxScore = sum of rewards); bounded-threshold = hit a target/wave/opponent; performance = score-attack, the number IS the goal. Completion prompts default to none/bounded-collectible, never performance.

scopeCut.description: What is deliberately OUT of v1 (4-8 entries recommended). The anti-slop guardrail; downstream treats these as out-of-bounds. Names systems, not one-off named features. Never cuts game-feel/juice. Distinguishes 'more systems' (cut) from 'a short engine-reuse level-ladder' (kept — SKILL §3 scope-preserve).

mustPreserve.description: The scope-PRESERVE counter-pressure (SKILL §3): the ONE core contested decision (a risk weighed against a reward on the critical path) the scope-cut must NOT remove, so W1 keeps round one a real game, not a tutorial. A relation in one line, never a per-prompt constant. Omit only for a pure sandbox prompt with no contested decision.

coreFantasy.description: Optional <=8-word emotional hook. Helps W1 + W3. Omit if not obvious.

lowConfidence.description: Optional convenience boolean mirroring confidence=='low'. The canonical signal is the confidence enum; emit this only if a consumer wants a plain boolean.
